title: 10k-action Frost versus Ninja performance

harness.command: FROST_BIN=<isolated-release-frost> python3 frost_bench.py run --suite standard --tools frost,ninja --sizes 10000 --iterations 5 --jobs 8
harness.comparison: identical frost_bench.py generated graph and commands

local_pre_change_diagnostic.note: Same-day diagnostic used to locate bottlenecks; not the issue acceptance baseline.

phase_profile.method: frost build --jobs 8 --stats on a fresh 10k generated workspace
remaining_clean_work: declared input stat/hash and action-key validation
output stat/hash and immutable CAS copy publication
remaining_noop_work: deserialize the graph, journal, and hash cache
parallel stat validation of all unique inputs and outputs

implemented_optimizations: skip repeated CAS scans with a bounded successful-GC stamp while retaining periodic cap recovery
reuse one append-only journal writer per build
validate a fully cached closure in parallel before constructing scheduler state
stream action keys directly into BLAKE3 and cache environment lookups
let a worker directly continue a newly-ready dependency-chain action
deduplicate output-directory creation and skip depfile bookkeeping for actions without depfiles
correctness_guards: CAS publication and restore use copies so workspace mutation cannot alter immutable objects
CAS objects are content-verified before restore
a restarted process scans and repairs a pre-existing over-budget CAS
the streamed action-key digest is tested against the canonical ActionKey implementation
